mnemonics: EGG-REP: Extended Global Get Regular Expression Print - for remembering egrep is the Extended version of grep
keyPoints: egrep supports extended regular expressions (EREs), which include meta-characters like +, ?, |, and () without needing to escape them
The vertical bar | acts as an OR operator, allowing for multiple pattern matching (e.g., 'error|warning|fail')
Parentheses () are used for grouping patterns to apply quantifiers to entire groups or for alternation within a group
commonMistakes: Forgetting that egrep treats metacharacters like +, ?, |, and () as special by default, unlike basic grep which requires escaping them with \
Using character classes incorrectly (e.g., [a-z] matches lowercase letters, but [a-z] in egrep doesn't need extra escapes, though confusing it with expanded groups like (abc|def) is common)
